title: Adapter (the kit↔runtime binding contract)
description: Two adapter species, discriminated by `kind`. A VALIDATION adapter binds a (layer, scope) to a concrete verifier command that mints a signal from its exit status — how a kit makes 'validated' mean something for its family. A DISPATCH adapter binds the server's dispatch boundary (§9.2) to a concrete headless agent runtime — how a worker is actually launched in a hermetic worktree under active gates. The kernel defines this contract; a kit/instance supplies the concrete binding (OD-003: the dispatch binding is pinned against current product docs at build time, never guessed). kit-conform validates every adapter against this schema.

kind.description: validation = run a verifier for a layer; dispatch = launch a worker runtime.

name.description: Adapter slug, unique within the kit.

target.description: VALIDATION only — which cells this adapter validates. Omitted axes are wildcards.

verifier.description: VALIDATION only — the command (argv) that validate.py runs; exit 0 = pass. Tokens {cell}, {asset}, {worktree} are substituted at dispatch. The worker cannot reach this command's reference material (pristine-reference scoring).

runtime.description: DISPATCH only — the agent runtime this adapter launches. `mock` is the deterministic test runtime used by Crawl/CI (no live model); the live bindings are pinned against current product docs (OD-003).

invocation.description: DISPATCH only — the command (argv) that launches the worker. Tokens {ticket}, {cell}, {worktree}, {skill_surface}, {budget} are substituted.

guarantees.description: DISPATCH only — runtime guarantees the contract REQUIRES (§9.2). kit-conform checks they are all asserted true.

hermetic_worktree.description: Runs in the provided isolated git worktree.

gates_active.description: PreToolUse/PostToolUse gates enforce protected paths inside the worktree.

event_stream.description: Emits tool/loop events the dispatcher tees into the ledger.
